planets.name: Love
planets.summary: Archive cluster around love: 1748 posts; 4 quote-backed receipts pinned.

artifacts.title: Love receipt from 2025-12-13
artifacts.excerpt:
There is, and it's closely related to a genera, which is the opposite of trauma. It's an event so intense it ruptures your sense of self and makes your old identity impossible to sustain. But instead of the difference between old and new you becoming a whirlpool of pain and rumination, the rupture becomes a wellspring through which your soul pours into the world. 

Examples include: Near-death experiences, finding the great love of your life, helping deliver a baby, meeting a hero and finding that he admires something in you that he doesn't have. Being forgiven by someone you once harmed and still carry immeasurable guilt for.  Creating a work of art so beautiful you can't believe it came through you. Finding you are still loved after a nervous breakdown. Meditating until reality dissolves into indescribably beautiful vibration. Leaving a lover better than you found them. Loving someone

artifacts.excerpt: Whenever I talk with someone about their lack of discipline, they inevitably reveal that they treat themselves like shit. And I ask them, if you were training any other mammal, what would you expect? Would you beat a dog and expect it to happily obey? Mastery requires love.

artifacts.title: Think receipt from 2025-12-17
artifacts.excerpt:
There aren't many things I think are genuinely evil, but perpetuating the lie that men often leave their wives when they get sick with cancer is evil.

Just so you all know, a cancer diagnosis for either sex decreases the risk of divorce, for both men and women.

It's worth thinking carefully about a social movement that feels justified in recklessly destroying the social fabric to achieve their "consciousness raising"

artifacts.title: Think receipt from 2025-09-21
artifacts.excerpt:
Before my son transitioned, we worked out a set of conditions together that he had to meet so that we could both feel confident it was the right move: 

1. No chemicals or surgery before 18. Period.

2. He needed to be able to dispassionately recite opposing positions, including difficult and critical perspectives, on the subjects of transgender identity and transition as a treatment for gender dysphoria.

3. He needed to fully research and understand the potential complications, including tail risks like deformation, lifelong pain and dependence on the medical industry

4. Two years of therapy dedicated to gender dysphoria issues to see if they could be resolved that way

5. A long weekend in the woods on solo meditation retreat, praying to God and communing with the deepest parts of the self to be sure this would be the right decision.

After doing all of these, and a lot of time for c
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: think, don, lot, way.

artifacts.title: Think receipt from 2025-09-06
artifacts.excerpt:
The ancient Greeks called the time of life between 27 and 33 "the period of ashes." It's still apt today. To extend the metaphor, around the mid-twenties, a young adult realizes the fire in their life has all but gone out, and their inner "stove" is clogged up with spiritual soot and charcoal - regrets, guilt, self-betrayal and usually a lot of shame. They need to go deep into themselves, clean out everything that's blocking them up, stop burning dirty fuel (acting from their conditioning, automatic conformity,) and discover what truly lights them up as individuals.

For astrologer-types, this is also known as the "Saturn return" period and the same ideas hold. NB: 70% of my following is in this age range (I think TPOT hews around here, minus a few outliers like me) so there appears to be some resonance between my writing and this time of life, but I only have a vague outline of it.
